feed.title: Docket updates for N.Z. v. Fenix International Limited, 8:24-cv-01655
feed.rights: Created for the public domain by Free Law Project

entry.title: Entry #251 in N.Z. v. Fenix International Limited, 8:24-cv-01655
entry.summary: MINUTES (IN CHAMBERS) by Judge Fred W. Slaughter: ORDER GRANTING IN PART AND DENYING IN PART DEFENDANTS' MOTIONS TO DISMISS PLAINTIFFS' SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT 225, 227, 228, 229, 230, 231, 232 . If Plaintiffs believe they can cure the deficiencies described in this Order, Plaintiffs shall file any amended complaint on or before June 9, 2026. If Plaintiffs do not file an amended complaint, Agency Defendants shall file an answer to the claims and allegations remaining in the SAC on or before June 23, 2026, and the court will dismiss Fenix Defendants from this case. SEE DOCUMENT FOR FURTHER INFORMATION. (twdb) (Entered: 05/21/2026)

entry.title: Minute entry from 2026-04-06 in N.Z. v. Fenix International Limited, 8:24-cv-01655
entry.summary: Dismiss AND Dismiss AND Dismiss AND Dismiss AND Dismiss AND Dismiss Party

entry.title: Entry #247 in N.Z. v. Fenix International Limited, 8:24-cv-01655
entry.summary: (IN CHAMBERS) ORDER by Judge Fred W. Slaughter: Before the court are several of Defendants' motions: (1) Fenix International Limited's and Fenix Internet LLC's Motion to Dismiss 225 ; (2) Elite Creators LLC's Motion to Dismiss 227 ; (3) Moxy Management's Motion to Dismiss 228 ; and (4) Verge Agency, Inc.'s Motion to Dismiss 229 . Unruly Agency LLC, Behave Agency LLC, and Content X, Inc. join in the foregoing motions. [230, 231, 232]. The court finds these matters appropriate for decision without oral argument. See Fed. R. Civ. P. 78(b); C.D. Cal. L.R. 7-15. Accordingly, the hearing set for April 9, 2026, at 10:00 a.m. in Courtroom 10D is VACATED and the court takes the matter UNDER SUBMISSION. THERE IS NO PDF DOCUMENT ASSOCIATED WITH THIS ENTRY. (rrp) TEXT ONLY ENTRY (Entered: 04/06/2026)

entry.title: Entry #246 in N.Z. v. Fenix International Limited, 8:24-cv-01655
entry.summary: Notice of Supplemental Authority filed by Plaintiffs A. L., B. L., R. M., S. M., N. Z.. (Attachments: # 1 Declaration of Richard Garcia-Almendros, # 2 Exhibit A to Garcia-Almendros Declaration)(Carey, Robert) (Entered: 04/03/2026)

entry.title: Minute entry from 2026-03-11 in N.Z. v. Fenix International Limited, 8:24-cv-01655
entry.summary: Text Only Scheduling Notice

entry.title: Entry #245 in N.Z. v. Fenix International Limited, 8:24-cv-01655
entry.summary: SCHEDULING NOTICE by Judge Fred W. Slaughter: On its own motion, the court CONTINUES the in-person hearing on the Motions to Dismiss, (Dkts. 225, 227-231), to April 9, 2026, at 10:00 a.m., in Courtroom 10D. See Mediterranean Enters., Inc. v. Ssangyong Corp., 708 F.2d 1458, 1465 (9th Cir. 1983) ("The trial court possesses the inherent power to control its own docket and calendar."); Thompson v. Hous. Auth. of City of Los Angeles, 782 F.2d 829, 831 (9th Cir. 1986) ("District courts have inherent power to control their dockets."). THERE IS NO PDF DOCUMENT ASSOCIATED WITH THIS ENTRY. (rrp) TEXT ONLY ENTRY (Entered: 03/11/2026)
